tasks.instruction: Dopasuj nazwy krajów anglojęzycznych (1–6) do opisów słynnych miejsc lub zabytków w tych krajach (A–H). Dwa opisy zostały podane dodatkowo i nie pasują do żadnego elementu.

options.text: This country is home to Uluru, a massive red sandstone rock sacred to the Aboriginal people, located in the Northern Territory.
The Statue of Liberty, a gift from France, stands on an island in the harbour of its largest city and is a symbol of freedom.
Stonehenge, a prehistoric circle of giant standing stones, can be found on Salisbury Plain in this country.
Table Mountain, a flat-topped mountain overlooking a major coastal city, is one of the most famous landmarks in this country.
The CN Tower in Toronto was once the world's tallest free-standing structure and dominates the skyline of this country's most populous city.
The Sydney Opera House, with its distinctive sail-shaped roofs, is located on a harbour in this country's largest city.
The Giant's Causeway, made of thousands of hexagonal basalt columns, is a natural wonder on the northern coast of this island.
Milford Sound, a dramatic fjord surrounded by steep cliffs and waterfalls, is one of the most visited natural sites in this country.
